---
title: "3.4 Constructors"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Constructors

### A constructor creates an object and establishes its starting state

Students already used constructor calls such as:

```java
new World(300, 300)
new Turtle(world)
new Person("Pat", "pat@gmail.com", "123-456-7890")
```

This topic moves from calling constructors to writing our own.

## Constructor Signature

### The constructor name must match the class name

```java
public Person(String initName, String initEmail, String initPhoneNumber)
{
    name = initName;
    email = initEmail;
    phoneNumber = initPhoneNumber;
}
```

::: {.tight-list}
- constructors are usually written after instance variables
- they have <span class="mark">no return type</span>, not even `void`
- the constructor name is the same as the class name
- the parameter list receives the arguments from the constructor call
:::

## The Job of a Constructor

### Put the object into a valid state

The textbook centers this topic on <span class="term">state</span>.

::: {.tight-list}
- an object's state is the collection of its instance-variable values at a given time
- a constructor should initialize those values to something <span class="term">useful</span>
- a useful starting point means the object is in a <span class="term">valid state</span>
- once constructed, the object should be ready for its public methods to run
:::

## Default Constructor

### Java can supply one only when you do not write your own

If a class has no written constructor, Java provides a <span class="term">default constructor</span> with no parameters.

| Type | Default value |
|---|---|
| `int` | `0` |
| `double` | `0.0` |
| `boolean` | `false` |
| object reference | `null` |

These defaults only work when they still leave the object in a valid state.

## Writing One Changes the Rule

### Once you define a constructor, Java stops generating the default one

::: {.tight-list}
- this protects the class from being created with missing required data
- `Person` should not be created without the values its design depends on
- if the class needs multiple useful entry points, write multiple constructors
- this is called <span class="term">overloading</span>
:::

Overloading is justified only when the extra constructor still creates valid objects.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- identify the signature rules for constructors
- explain why constructors should initialize all important instance variables
- compare default-constructor behavior with custom initialization
- evaluate whether a constructor leaves an object in a valid state
- <span class="term">3.4.3 Coding Challenge: Student Class</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- state that a constructor has the same name as the class
- explain that constructors have no return type
- define object <span class="term">state</span> and <span class="term">valid state</span>
- explain when Java provides a default constructor
- describe what changes after a class defines its own constructor
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
